mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, consumer spending power collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns as equity valuations adjust to recessionary labor market conditions.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based consumer confidence and credit availability, driving sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter as earnings growth accelerates.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term loses explanatory power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which compresses equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week during early recovery phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of rising INDPRO observations, or the mechanism shows zero predictive power during confirmed recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 2.5 percent year-over-year, inflation expectations force central banks to tighten policy, increasing discount rates and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns as growth concerns dominate.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in over 65% of high-inflation threshold crossings, or the negative correlation reverses during inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and stagnant unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening and wage inflation pressures, which compress corporate profit margins and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The divergence signal predicts positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real gross domestic product reflect expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, which directly translate into positive S&P 500 forward returns as equity markets price in higher earnings trajectories.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of GDP expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 95 percent of its baseline, manufacturing contraction signals imminent supply chain disruptions and earnings downgrades, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of threshold breaches, or the signal fails to maintain predictive accuracy during contractionary supply regimes.
